acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI/integration tests no longer import daemon internals directly
- [ ] Daemon-internal tests live under daemon-owned boundaries
- [ ] Critical integration suites still pass
description:
**Problem**
Integration suites still import `beads_rs::daemon::*`, which will break crate extraction.
design: Move tests/fixtures to surface/daemon-core boundaries and keep daemon-internal coverage in daemon-owned test modules.
title: Migrate integration tests/fixtures off daemon-internal imports

description:
**Problem**
Metrics snapshot state is process-global, which makes strict counter/histogram assertions in concurrently running tests brittle.

**Design**
Add a test-only metrics reset/scope helper so unit tests can assert exact deltas deterministically without depending on process-global accumulation.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Test-only reset/scope API available under cfg(test)
- [ ] Existing sync/daemon metrics tests use deterministic local baselines
- [ ] No production behavior changes

**Files:** crates/beads-daemon/src/metrics.rs, crates/beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs
title: Provide test-only metrics reset/scope API to avoid cross-test interference

description:
**Problem**
In `src/git/collision.rs`, collision resolution remaps the loser and writes a tombstone for the old ID using a "resolution stamp". But the merge rule in `core/state.rs` is: tombstone wins unless the beads `updated_stamp()` is strictly newer.

If the tombstone stamp is newer than the winner beads updated stamp (likely, since resolution stamp is generated "now"), **the winner gets deleted during join**. This violates spec §4.1.1 ("winner retains original id") and is catastrophic.

**Design Options**

*Option A (cleanest):* Lineage-scoped tombstones
Tombstone includes the losing beads `created` stamp (or content-hash) so it only kills that lineage, not the winner occupying the ID.

*Option B (minimal change):* Emit two stamps on collision resolution
- `t_del` for the loser-id tombstone  
- `t_win` for a no-op "bump" on the winner bead so `t_win > t_del`
This makes the winner survive the delete rule while preventing loser resurrection.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Deterministic test: collision where winner is older, loser is newer, resolution happens later
- [ ] Winner survives resolution in all timestamp orderings
- [ ] Loser cannot resurrect after resolution
- [ ] Spec compliance verified

**Files:** src/git/collision.rs, src/core/state.rs
title: Fix collision resolution: winner can be deleted by resolution tombstone
